children.name: Oreoica
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crested Bellbird, a medium-sized, crest-bearing passerine endemic to Australia. It is named for its extraordinary, ventriloquial song that sounds like a clear, ringing bell from a distance, making its exact location difficult to pinpoint. It inhabits dry woodlands and shrublands, foraging on the ground for insects and seeds.

children.name: Ornorectes
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crested Pitohui, a robust, thrush-sized passerine endemic to the lowland forests of New Guinea. It is one of the few known poisonous bird genera, with its skin and feathers containing batrachotoxin, the same toxin found in poison dart frogs. It has a distinctive bright orange crest that it raises when excited.

children.name: Pseudorectes
children.description: A genus of moderate-sized, thrush-like passerines endemic to New Guinea, formerly placed with the whistlers but now recognised as part of the bellbird family. They have robust bodies, strong legs, and a stocky build adapted for a largely terrestrial or near-ground foraging lifestyle. Their plumage is predominantly brown and rufous with subtle barring.
